name: Sugar Mixtures Raman Dataset (Low SNR)
description: The low signal-to-noise ratio subset of the Sugar Mixtures benchmark (7,680 measurements at 0.5 s integration). Used for evaluating the noise-robustness of hyperspectral unmixing and quantification algorithms.
citeAs: @article{Georgiev_2024, title={Hyperspectral unmixing for Raman spectroscopy via physics-constrained autoencoders}, volume={121}, ISSN={1091-6490}, url={http://dx.doi.org/10.1073/pnas.2407439121}, DOI={10.1073/pnas.2407439121}, number={45}, journal={Proceedings of the National Academy of Sciences}, publisher={Proceedings of the National Academy of Sciences}, author={Georgiev, Dimitar and Fernandez-Galiana, Alvaro and Vilms Pedersen, Simon and Papadopoulos, Georgios and Xie, Ruoxiao and Stevens, Molly M. and Barahona, Mauricio}, year={2024}, month=oct}

distribution.name: Sugar Mixtures Raman Dataset (Low SNR)
distribution.description: Source data hosted at the original repository.

field.name: spectrum_id
field.description: Sequential integer index identifying each spectrum.

field.name: raman_shift_cm_inv
field.description: Raman shift wavenumber axis in cm⁻¹. Shape: (n_wavenumbers,).

field.name: spectrum_intensity
field.description: Raman scattering intensity in arbitrary units. Shape: (n_wavenumbers,) per spectrum.

field.name: target
field.description: Target value(s): concentration, physical property, or other continuous measurement.

rai:dataCollection: Raman spectra acquired at 0.5 s integration time using a custom B-Raman microspectroscopy platform (Thorlabs Cerna body with BWTek BRM-785-0.55-100-0.22-SMA laser excitation source at 785 nm and 36.3 mW, and Ibsen EAGLE RAMAN-S spectrometer). Spectra collected in reflection via a Leica N PLAN 10x/0.25 objective (0.25 NA) over wavenumbers 142–3684.8 cm⁻¹. A library of 240 aqueous sugar mixture solutions was prepared in 96-well plates (375 µL per well) covering a full factorial design of four sugar types at four volume levels each; five additional pure-solution wells provided reference endmember spectra. Measurements repeated at eight positions per well with four repetitions, yielding 7,840 spectra (7,840 including reference spectra).
rai:dataPreprocessingProtocol: Spectra cropped to 400–1,800 cm⁻¹; baseline correction using Adaptive Smoothness Parameter Penalized Least Squares (ASPLS) with smoothing parameter lambda = 1e5, differential matrix order 2, maximum 100 iterations, and exit-criteria tolerance 0.001; global vector normalization dividing each spectrum by its maximum observed intensity. Standard spectral angle divergence (SAD) loss used for model training without the additional MSE abundance term applied in the high-SNR variant.
rai:dataAnnotationProtocol: Ground-truth endmember signatures obtained from the median of reference spectra collected from five additional pure-solution wells (water, sucrose, fructose, maltose, glucose at 375 µL each; 32 reference spectra per component in the low-SNR setup). Ground-truth fractional abundances determined from the volume ratios of the components present in each mixture. No supervised concentration labels are used; unmixing performance is evaluated against these reference-derived ground truths.
rai:personalSensitiveInformation: No personal or sensitive information is included. All spectra originate from aqueous sugar solutions without individual human identifiers.
rai:dataBiases: Dataset collected on a single custom B-Raman microspectroscopy platform; spectral characteristics are instrument-specific and may not transfer directly to other Raman systems. Only four sugar species (sucrose, fructose, maltose, glucose) are represented; models trained on this data may not generalise to chemical systems with more endmembers or different spectral properties. The short integration time (0.5 s) introduces substantial shot noise and baseline artefacts; conventional unmixing methods are known to degrade significantly under these conditions, which may bias benchmarking results in favour of noise-robust approaches. Linear mixing is assumed; bilinear or nonlinear interactions are not modelled.
rai:dataLimitations: Only four sugar species at a limited set of discrete volume levels; models may not generalise to broader chemical diversity or continuously varying concentrations. Ground-truth fractional abundances are derived from volume ratios rather than independent analytical measurements such as HPLC. The low SNR (0.5 s integration) causes the spectra to exhibit substantial noise, baseline variations, and cosmic spikes; this makes endmember identification challenging and increases the difficulty of the benchmark relative to the high-SNR variant. Linear mixing is assumed throughout; bilinear or post-nonlinear interactions are not modelled.
rai:dataSocialImpact: Provides a challenging low-SNR experimental benchmark for evaluating the noise robustness of unsupervised and self-supervised hyperspectral unmixing algorithms in Raman spectroscopy. Demonstrates the advantage of autoencoder-based methods over conventional approaches when data quality is limited, with relevance to rapid screening, in-line monitoring, and portable Raman applications where long integration times are impractical.
rai:dataUseCases: Benchmarking hyperspectral unmixing algorithms under realistic low-SNR conditions; evaluation of noise robustness in endmember extraction and fractional abundance estimation; paired comparison with the high-SNR variant to study signal-to-noise effects; development and testing of denoising-aware autoencoder architectures for Raman spectroscopy.
